ItacWorkItem.ShortTitle: Alexandri Carerii Patavini Iuris Utriusque Doctoris Peripateticae Sacraeque Sapientiae studiosi
ItacWorkItem.LongTitle: De somnis deque Divinatione per somnia brevis Consideratio. Ad Reverendissimus Ascanium Marchesinum Episcopum Maiorensem Dominum et patronum colendiss[imum]
ItacWorkItem.Notes: According to Tomasini, `Elogia`, p. 363, Carriero delivered this lesson at the Accademia degli Animosi. This is confirmed in the Dedication. According to Olivieri Secchi, `Carriero, Alessandro`, in DBI, in this publication the author attempts a reconciliation between the Aristotelian interpretation of dreams, and theologians` teachings. Thus, dreams are sent to selected human beings by God, through nature. This went against Plato`s and Avicenna`s theory, according to which dreams are innate.
ItacWorkItem.CensorsAgreement: Fr. Bonaventura Manentus Gabianus Vic. Rever. P. Inquisitoris Paduae vidit et fidem facit nihil contineri contra fidem, contra principes aut contra bonos mores, et permittit impressioni dari.
ItacWorkItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
